post.summary: Generate category clusters with recursive splitting
post.description:
Generate category clusters from user questions with automatic recursive splitting
    for large clusters. Clusters are ranked by half-life weightage (recency).

    **Features:**
    - Automatically splits clusters larger than max_cluster_size
    - Randomly shuffles cluster members before each split iteration
    - Supports up to 5 recursive split iterations (configurable)
    - Multiple clustering methods: agglomerative, dbscan, hierarchical
    - Azure OpenAI or local embeddings
    - Optional named entity removal and re-clustering (batch optimized)
    - Optional save to database and Elasticsearch

    **Entity Removal:**
    When enabled, removes named entities (companies, people, dates, etc.) from questions
    and re-clusters based on intent, merging similar questions like:
    - "Show me Tata Motors" + "Show me Apple" → "Show me {{company_name}}"
    Uses batch processing (100 questions/call) for 100x reduction in API calls.

schema.description: Number of top clusters to return
schema.title: Top K

schema.title: Clustering Method

post.summary: Generate category and subcategory for a list of questions
post.description:
Generate category and subcategory for a list of questions.
    
    **Features:**
    - Clusters similar questions and generates category/subcategory pairs
    - Validates generated pairs against existing categories in KBMS
    - Optionally saves intent candidates to KBMS database for review
    - Filters out candidates similar to existing pending intent candidates
    
    **Save to DB:**
    When save_to_db is enabled, intent candidates are created in KBMS with:
    - CREATE_NEW suggestion if no similar existing category found
    - USE_EXISTING suggestion with matched category/subcategory IDs if similar exists
    
    **Similarity Filtering:**
    Before saving, candidates are compared against existing pending intent candidates
    using vector similarity. Candidates with similarity >= threshold are filtered out
    to avoid duplicates.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of questions to fetch from database
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of questions to fetch from database

parameters.name: save_to_db

schema.description: Save generated intent candidates to KBMS database for review
schema.title: Save To Db

parameters.description: Save generated intent candidates to KBMS database for review

parameters.name: intent_similarity_threshold

schema.description: Similarity threshold (0.0-1.0) for filtering duplicate intent candidates against existing pending ones. Candidates with similarity >= threshold are not saved.
schema.title: Intent Similarity Threshold

parameters.description: Similarity threshold (0.0-1.0) for filtering duplicate intent candidates against existing pending ones. Candidates with similarity >= threshold are not saved.

post.description:
Assemble market data and generate the Opening Bell transcript via LLM.

Steps:
1. Fetch today's market briefing from the DB
2. Fetch last N days of news from the DB
3. Fetch same-day headlines from live ET RSS
4. Send everything to the LLM
5. Return structured JSON transcript

post.summary: Run the full Opening Bell pipeline end-to-end
post.description:
Run four steps in order and return narration text + audio download URL.

Steps executed:
1. Sync Economic Times RSS feed
2. Generate market briefing
3. Assemble data, generate LLM transcript, and produce TTS audio
4. Publish audio (local download URL or S3)

When email=true or slack=true, also sends notifications (async by default).
On failure the response includes per-step diagnostics so callers know
exactly which step broke and why.

parameters.name: news_days

schema.description: Number of days of historical news to include
schema.title: News Days

parameters.description: Number of days of historical news to include

schema.description: Date of the market briefing to use (defaults to today)

get.summary: Download a previously generated Opening Bell audio file
get.description:
Serve the WAV file produced by a previous /run-full-pipeline call.

Pass the ``audio_path`` value from the pipeline response as the query
parameter. No audio is regenerated — the file must already exist on disk.

Document.title: Document
Document.description:
Class for storing a piece of text and associated metadata.

Example:

    .. code-block:: python

        from langchain_core.documents import Document

        document = Document(
            page_content="Hello, world!", metadata={"source": "https://example.com"}
        )
